message:
So last week our power went out for like four hours, and I learned something hilarious about myself: I'd somehow convinced myself I was *totally* prepared. Spoiler alert—I wasn't.

I had a flashlight. Singular. In a junk drawer. The battery was corroded. My phone was at 12%. And I stood in my kitchen genuinely shocked that I couldn't just… magic up light and cold food.

The funny part? I kept saying "I should really get one of those lanterns." Like I was waiting for permission or something.

Turns out being ready doesn't have to be complicated—just a couple of flashlights that actually work, maybe a power bank charging somewhere, and knowing where your blankets are. That's it. Not doomsday stuff. Just "oh, I thought of this, so it's fine" stuff.

Anyway, this little refresher helped me actually *do* something about it instead of just thinking about it.

What's one prep thing you've been meaning to grab but keep putting off? 😄
